title: SyncSender.ok() silently drops events under backpressure with no documentation
description: send_event() calls tx.send(event).ok(), discarding the Result. The SyncSender capacity is 256. When the UI is slow to drain (e.g. during a long repaint), the send blocks until space is available — but the .ok() makes it appear non-blocking to reviewers. There is no comment explaining that this is intentional drop-on-full behavior vs. a deliberate backpressure acceptance. A contributor will copy this pattern for new events without understanding that events can be silently lost during bursts.
